text:
(a) A provision of this chapter that requires a vehicle to be equipped with fixed electric lights does not apply to a farm trailer or fertilizer trailer registered under Section 502.146 (Certain Farm Vehicles and Drilling and Construction Equipment) or a boat trailer with a gross weight of 3,000 pounds or less if the trailer is not operated at a time or under a condition specified by Section 547.302 (Duty to Display Lights) (a).

(b) Except for Sections 547.323 (Stoplamps Required) and 547.324 (Turn Signal Lamps Required) , a provision of this chapter that requires a vehicle to be equipped with fixed electric lights does not apply to a boat trailer with a gross weight of less than 4,500 pounds if the trailer is not operated at a time or under a condition specified by Section 547.302 (Duty to Display Lights) (a).

(c) Except for Sections 547.323 (Stoplamps Required) and 547.324 (Turn Signal Lamps Required) , a provision of this chapter that requires a vehicle to be equipped with lamps, reflectors, and lighting equipment does not apply to a mobile home if the mobile home:

(1) is moved under a permit issued by the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles under Subchapter D (Nonapplicability of Subchapter) , Chapter 623 (Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles) ; and

(2) is not moved at a time or under a condition specified by Section 547.302 (Duty to Display Lights) (a).

(d) A mobile home lighted as provided by this section may be moved only during daytime.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 547. Vehicle Equip.
##### § 547.304 — Applicability

(a) A provision of this chapter that requires a vehicle to be equipped with fixed electric lights does not apply to a farm trailer or fertilizer trailer registered under Section 502.146 (Certain Farm Vehicles and Drilling and Construction Equipment) or a boat trailer with a gross weight of 3,000 pounds or less if the trailer is not operated at a time or under a condition specified by Section 547.302 (Duty to Display Lights) (a).

(b) Except for Sections 547.323 (Stoplamps Required) and 547.324 (Turn Signal Lamps Required) , a provision of this chapter that requires a vehicle to be equipped with fixed electric lights does not apply to a boat trailer with a gross weight of less than 4,500 pounds if the trailer is not operated at a time or under a condition specified by Section 547.302 (Duty to Display Lights) (a).

(c) Except for Sections 547.323 (Stoplamps Required) and 547.324 (Turn Signal Lamps Required) , a provision of this chapter that requires a vehicle to be equipped with lamps, reflectors, and lighting equipment does not apply to a mobile home if the mobile home:

    (1) is moved under a permit issued by the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles under Subchapter D (Nonapplicability of Subchapter) , Chapter 623 (Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles) ; and

    (2) is not moved at a time or under a condition specified by Section 547.302 (Duty to Display Lights) (a).

(d) A mobile home lighted as provided by this section may be moved only during daytime.

*Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 161 (S.B.1093), Sec. 20.021, eff. September 1, 2013.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A provision of this chapter that requires a vehicle to be equipped with fixed electric lights does not apply to a farm trailer or fertilizer trailer registered under Section 502.146 (Certain Farm Vehicles and Drilling and Construction Equipment) or a boat trailer with a gross weight of 3,000 pounds or less if the trailer is not operated at a time or under a condition specified by Section 547.302 (Duty to Display Lights) (a).

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Except for Sections 547.323 (Stoplamps Required) and 547.324 (Turn Signal Lamps Required) , a provision of this chapter that requires a vehicle to be equipped with fixed electric lights does not apply to a boat trailer with a gross weight of less than 4,500 pounds if the trailer is not operated at a time or under a condition specified by Section 547.302 (Duty to Display Lights) (a).

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: Except for Sections 547.323 (Stoplamps Required) and 547.324 (Turn Signal Lamps Required) , a provision of this chapter that requires a vehicle to be equipped with lamps, reflectors, and lighting equipment does not apply to a mobile home if the mobile home:
is moved under a permit issued by the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles under Subchapter D (Nonapplicability of Subchapter) , Chapter 623 (Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles) ; and
is not moved at a time or under a condition specified by Section 547.302 (Duty to Display Lights) (a).

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: A mobile home lighted as provided by this section may be moved only during daytime.
